question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidatorImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: A bug in the respective layer 0/1/2 network code that results in unintended smart contract behavior with no concrete funds at direct risk'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the validate() method does not validate that the filename recorded from the first signature matches the filenames of subsequent signatures, so that a mixed-filename collection could pass consensus validation under the first file's name, causing the mirror node to associate consensus approval with the wrong stream file and ingest incorrect smart contract execution results?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidatorImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: A bug in the respective layer 0/1/2 network code that results in unintended smart contract behavior with no concrete funds at direct risk'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the signatureHashMap uses fileHashAsHex (a hex-encoded string) as the grouping key, and if DomainUtils.bytesToHex() produces an empty string for a zero-length fileHash byte array, all signatures with empty fileHash would be grouped together under the empty string key, potentially allowing a set of signatures with empty/invalid hashes to collectively satisfy canReachConsensus() and cause the mirror node to accept a stream file with no verifiable content hash?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidatorImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: A bug in the respective layer 0/1/2 network code that results in unintended smart contract behavior with no concrete funds at direct risk'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the validate() method does not enforce a minimum number of signatures required for consensus, so that a single VERIFIED signature from a node whose stake alone meets the stakeRequiredForConsensus threshold (e.g., a node with more than 1/3 of total stake) can unilaterally cause the mirror node to accept any stream file, including one with manipulated smart contract state, without any cross-node agreement?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidatorImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: A bug in the respective layer 0/1/2 network code that results in unintended smart contract behavior with no concrete funds at direct risk'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the validate() method does not check for negative stake values returned by signature.getNode().getStake(), so that a node reporting a negative stake could cause the stake accumulation (stake += signature.getNode().getStake()) to underflow, making canReachConsensus() return false for a legitimate consensus group and blocking valid smart contract stream files from being ingested?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidatorImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: A bug in the respective layer 0/1/2 network code that results in unintended smart contract behavior with no concrete funds at direct risk'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the validate() method does not check for negative totalStake values from signature.getNode().getTotalStake(), so that a node reporting a negative totalStake causes getStakeRequiredForConsensus() to compute a negative required stake, making canReachConsensus() trivially return true for any non-negative stake value, allowing any single VERIFIED signature to satisfy consensus and accept a fraudulent smart contract stream file?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidatorImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: A bug in the respective layer 0/1/2 network code that results in unintended smart contract behavior with no concrete funds at direct risk'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the validate() method processes all signatures in a single pass without atomicity guarantees, so that if the underlying collection is modified concurrently during iteration (e.g., by another thread adding or removing signatures), a ConcurrentModificationException or silent data corruption could cause the consensus check to be performed on an incomplete or inconsistent set of signatures, leading to incorrect smart contract stream file acceptance?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidatorImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: A bug in the respective layer 0/1/2 network code that results in unintended smart contract behavior with no concrete funds at direct risk'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the validate() method does not verify that the SignatureStatus.VERIFIED status was set by a legitimate cryptographic verification process (NodeSignatureVerifier.verifySignature()), so that if a signature's status is manually set to VERIFIED without actual cryptographic verification, it would be counted in the consensus check and could contribute to accepting a fraudulent smart contract stream file?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidatorImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: A bug in the respective layer 0/1/2 network code that results in unintended smart contract behavior with no concrete funds at direct risk'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the validate() method uses consensusCount to track the number of signatures that reached consensus, but consensusCount is only incremented by validatedSignatures.size() (the count of signatures in the winning hash group), not by the number of distinct nodes, so that duplicate signatures from the same node in the same hash group inflate consensusCount and make the consensusCount > 0 check pass even with a single node's signature?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidatorImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: A bug in the respective layer 0/1/2 network code that results in unintended smart contract behavior with no concrete funds at direct risk'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the validate() method does not validate the StreamFilename object retrieved from the first signature, so that a null or malformed filename causes a NullPointerException or incorrect string formatting in the SignatureVerificationException message at line 84-85, potentially masking the true cause of consensus failure and allowing a retry with a fraudulent stream file to succeed?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidatorImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: A bug in the respective layer 0/1/2 network code that results in unintended smart contract behavior with no concrete funds at direct risk'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the validate() method's early return for zero consensusRatio (line 54-57) only checks !signatureHashMap.isEmpty() but does not verify that the signatures in the map are for the correct stream file, so that a single VERIFIED signature for any file (even a fraudulent one) triggers the early return and bypasses all consensus checks when consensusRatio is zero, causing the mirror node to accept a stream file with manipulated smart contract execution results?
